---
title: "GGPlot Practice"
subtitle: "Bivariate Data"
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
    embed-resources: true
---
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE, error = TRUE)
```


# Poverty and Crime

    1. Determine which is the explanatory variable (x) and which is the response variable (y)  
    2. Use `ggplot()` to make a scatter plot looking at the relationship between violent crime and poverty percentage.  
    3. Include a trend line.  

## Read in the data

```{r}

library(tidyverse)
library(mosaic)
library(rio)
library(car)


data <- import('https://online.stat.psu.edu/stat462/sites/onlinecourses.science.psu.edu.stat462/files/data/poverty/index.txt') %>% tibble()



```


## Create the Plot

```{r}


```


# Extra Credit:

Remove the outlier with ViolCrime > 60 and redo the graph: 

```{r}

```
